evidence_records.raw_value: Reuters photo wire: Zelenskyy at NATO summit in The Hague, June 27, 2025. Dark navy blazer, white collared shirt, matching dark navy trousers. Multiple angles published. Caption: 'Zelenskyy attends NATO leaders meeting.'
evidence_records.notes: Named resolution source per market rules. Clear formal attire. Question: does blazer = suit jacket?

evidence_records.raw_value: AP photo: 'Ukrainian President Zelenskyy arrives at The Hague in formal attire, a notable departure from his wartime military-green look.' Full-length photo shows matching dark ensemble.
evidence_records.notes: Named resolution source per market rules. AP uses term 'formal attire' not 'suit.'

evidence_records.raw_value: Getty Images editorial: High-resolution photos (3 angles). Professional fashion consultant quoted in metadata: 'The fabric on the jacket and trousers appears to be the same material and color — consistent with a two-piece suit.'
evidence_records.notes: Named resolution source per market rules. Expert analysis suggests matching suit.

evidence_records.raw_value: BBC World: 'Whether Zelenskyy's attire qualifies as a suit depends on interpretation. A blazer is not technically a suit jacket in fashion terminology — suits traditionally have matching fabric cut from the same bolt. The photos are inconclusive on this detail.'
evidence_records.notes: Not a named resolution source. Raises legitimate definitional ambiguity.

evidence_records.raw_value: Polymarket resolved NO. UMA governance vote: 2 whales controlling >50% of voting power voted NO. Final vote was near-unanimous (95%+) due to UMA's follow-the-majority incentive structure.
evidence_records.notes: NOT a resolution source. Market outcome driven by concentrated token voting, not evidence assessment.
